theme.title: Direct and Indirect Objects
theme.description: Practice direct object pronouns, indirect object pronouns, and combinations of both in practical Spanish.

lesson.title: Lesson 4: Practical Mixed Sentences
lesson.instructions: Work with object pronouns in shopping, work, family, and daily-life situations.

exercises.prompt: Mi jefe me lo explico con mucha paciencia.
exercises.answer: My boss explained it to me with a lot of patience.
exercises.explanation: 'Me lo' combines the receiver and the thing explained.

exercises.prompt: I bought the tickets for Ana, and I already sent them to her.
exercises.answer: Compre los boletos para Ana y ya se los mande.
exercises.answer_alt: Compre las entradas para Ana y ya se las mande.
exercises.explanation: The second clause uses both an indirect and a direct object pronoun.

exercises.prompt: Si quieres, te la preparo ahora.
exercises.answer: If you want, I will prepare it for you now.
exercises.explanation: 'Te la' means 'for you' plus a feminine singular object.

exercises.prompt: The waiter brought us the menu, but he still has not brought it to the next table.
exercises.answer: El camarero nos trajo el menu, pero todavia no se lo ha traido a la mesa de al lado.
exercises.explanation: The second clause uses 'se lo' because 'le lo' becomes 'se lo'.

exercises.prompt: No les voy a ofrecer eso porque ya lo tienen.
exercises.answer: I am not going to offer that to them because they already have it.
exercises.explanation: This sentence uses an indirect object pronoun and then a separate direct object pronoun.

exercises.prompt: My sister tells me the story every year, and every year she tells it to me differently.
exercises.answer: Mi hermana me cuenta la historia cada ano y cada ano me la cuenta de manera diferente.
exercises.explanation: 'Me la' replaces 'la historia' as the thing told to me.

exercises.prompt: Quiero mostrarselo antes de la reunion.
exercises.answer: I want to show it to him before the meeting.
exercises.answer_alt: I want to show it to her before the meeting.
exercises.explanation: Attached pronouns often appear with infinitives.

exercises.prompt: They wrote us a message, but they did not send it to us until late at night.
exercises.answer: Nos escribieron un mensaje, pero no nos lo mandaron hasta muy tarde en la noche.
